entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's coaching company; should focus on identifying external validation early to accelerate client level-up

entities.name: Internal self-assessment lag
entities.context: Core belief that self-perception systematically lags behind actual competence and market value

entities.name: Inverted imposter syndrome
entities.context: Framework where individual's output and capability precedes their identity update

entities.name: External market signals
entities.context: Objective validation from high-status entities (tier-one institutions, peer recognition) that forces self-model recalculation

entities.name: Readiness as lagging indicator
entities.context: Insight that feeling 'ready' comes after capability exists; implies targeting higher-tier opportunities before feeling qualified

entities.name: Self-perception anchoring
entities.context: Psychological tendency to anchor self-assessment to historical evidence to avoid identity shift costs

relationships.context: Coaching strategy should leverage this insight to identify external validation early
Inverted imposter syndrome is the named framework for the self-assessment lag phenomenon
External signals provide objective data that overcomes the lag in self-assessment
Readiness feeling lags actual capability, implying action should precede confidence
Anchoring to historical evidence is the psychological mechanism causing the lag
